definitions.definition: Electric potential is the work done per unit charge in bringing a charge from infinity to a point in an electric field. It is a scalar quantity and is measured in volts (V).
definitions.example: The electric potential at the surface of the Earth is taken as zero, and the potential at a point 1 meter above the ground is 100 V, then the work done in bringing a charge of 0.1 C from the ground to the point 1 meter above the ground is 10 J.

definitions.definition: The difference in electric potential between two points in an electric field is called electric potential difference or voltage. It is measured in volts (V).
definitions.example: The electric potential difference between two points in a circuit is 10 V, meaning that 10 J of work is required to move 1 C of charge from one point to the other.

definitions.definition: A potential difference is the same as electric potential difference. It is the difference in electric potential between two points in an electric field.
definitions.example: The potential difference between two points in a circuit is 10 V, meaning that 10 J of work is required to move 1 C of charge from one point to the other.

definitions.definition: The potential energy of a charge in an electric field is given by U = qV, where U is the potential energy, q is the charge, and V is the electric potential.
definitions.example: A charge of 0.1 C is placed at a point with an electric potential of 100 V. The potential energy of the charge is 10 J.

definitions.definition: The capacitance of a conductor is the ratio of the charge on it to the potential difference between its terminals. It is measured in farads (F).
definitions.example: A capacitor has a capacitance of 10 F. If a potential difference of 10 V is applied across it, the charge on the capacitor is 100 C.

definitions.definition: A device that stores electric charge is called a capacitor. It consists of two conductors separated by a dielectric material.
definitions.example: A capacitor is used to filter out high-frequency signals in a circuit.

definitions.definition: A dielectric material is a non-conductor of electricity. It is used as a medium between the conductors of a capacitor.
definitions.example: Air is a good dielectric material and is used as the medium between the conductors of a capacitor.

definitions.definition: The dielectric constant of a material is the ratio of the electric field of a capacitor with a particular dielectric to the electric field with air between the conductors.
definitions.example: The dielectric constant of air is 1, and that of glass is 3.5.

definitions.definition: A capacitor consisting of two parallel plates separated by a dielectric material is called a parallel plate capacitor.
definitions.example: A parallel plate capacitor is used in many electronic devices, including radios and televisions.

definitions.definition: A capacitor connected in series with other components in a circuit is called a series capacitor.
definitions.example: A series capacitor is used to filter out low-frequency signals in a circuit.

definitions.definition: The opposition to the flow of current in a capacitor is called capacitive reactance. It is measured in ohms (Ω).
definitions.example: The capacitive reactance of a capacitor is 100 Ω, meaning that the current flowing through it is 1 A when a potential difference of 100 V is applied across it.

definitions.definition: The ability of a capacitor to store charge is called charge storage.
definitions.example: A capacitor has a capacitance of 10 F and a charge storage of 100 C.

definitions.definition: When a capacitor is connected to a circuit and the charge on it flows out, it is called capacitive discharge.
definitions.example: A capacitor is connected to a circuit and the charge on it flows out, causing a current to flow in the circuit.

definitions.definition: The farad is the unit of capacitance. It is defined as the capacitance of a capacitor that stores a charge of 1 C when a potential difference of 1 V is applied across it.
definitions.example: A capacitor has a capacitance of 10 F, meaning that it can store a charge of 10 C when a potential difference of 1 V is applied across it.

definitions.definition: The electric field is a vector field that surrounds charged particles and exerts a force on other charged particles.
definitions.example: The electric field around a charged particle is proportional to the charge on the particle.
